content: GitHub API idempotency: Team already exists returns HTTP 422 with message 'Validation Failed' and errors[0].message like 'Name has already been taken'. Repository already exists returns HTTP 422 with errors[0].message 'name already exists on this account'. Member already in team returns HTTP 422. None of these are HTTP 409 — they are 422. Prevention pattern: catch 422, parse errors array for 'already' keywords, treat as idempotent success. Alternative: call GET /orgs/{org}/teams/{team_slug} first and only POST if 404. The GET-then-POST pattern is two API calls but produces cleaner code. For repos: GET /repos/{owner}/{repo} — 404 means create, 200 means already exists. For members: GET /orgs/{org}/teams/{slug}/memberships/{username} — if active or pending, treat as success.
